questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Her inquisitive nature led her to pursue a career in scientific research.
questions.explanation: D) Inquisitive(adjective) – curious, probing, questioning, prying, investigative जिज्ञासु Antonym: Indifferent(adjective) – Uninterested, unconcerned (उदासीन) Curious(adjective) – Eager to know (उत्सुक) Investigative(adjective) – Examining, probing (खोजी) Probing(adjective) – Inquiring, searching (जाँच करने वाला)

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the underlined word in the given sentence. We do not expet Reema to follow all the rules from the first day of work.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling of the underlined word ‘expet’is ‘expect’, which means “to think or believe that something will happen or someone will do something” (उम्मीद करना, अपेक्षा करना).

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error I have (A) / made the cupcakes so (B) / please hand out them (C) / to me (D)
questions.explanation: A) 'hand out them' के बदले 'hand them out' का use होगा क्योंकि जब Verb के साथ Preposition (out, in, up, down, etc.) जुड़ा हो और Object Pronoun (them, me, him, her, it, us, you) हो, तो Pronoun, Verb और Preposition के बीच में आता है; जैसे— • Pick the books up → Pick them up (✓) • Turn the light off → Turn it off (✓) 'hand them out' will be used instead of 'hand out them' because when a preposition (out, in, up, down, etc.) is attached to a verb and the object is a pronoun (them, me, him, her, it, us, you), the pronoun comes between the verb and the preposition; Like— • Pick the books up → Pick them up (✓) • Turn the light off → Turn it off (✓)

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who travels from place to place, especially for work
questions.explanation: A)Itinerant(noun) – A person who travels from place to place, especially for work.स्थान-स्थान पर काम की तलाश में घूमने वाला व्यक्ति। Vagrant(noun) – A person without a settled home or regular work who wanders from place to place. आवारा व्यक्ति, Nomad(noun) – A member of a group of people who move from one place to another rather than living in one place all the time. खानाबदोश, Pilgrim(noun) – A person who travels to a sacred place for religious reasons. तीर्थयात्री,

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Select the option that arranges the sentences in the correct logical sequence to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. a. This figure is predicted to climb as the percentage of Americans residing in rural areas falls by an average of 1.6% annually. b. The United Nations reports that India’s urban population is growing at a rate of 1.1 percent annually, while the country's rural population is shrinking at a rate of 0.37 percent annually. c. While 82% of Americans live in urban settings, only 30% of Indians do so. d. It's still a considerably smaller percentage of the Indian population than it is in the US that resides in urban areas.
questions.explanation: D)b, d, c, a The United Nations reports that India’s urban population is growing at a rate of 1.1 percent annually, while the country's rural population is shrinking at a rate of 0.37 percent annually It's still a considerably smaller percentage of the Indian population than it is in the US that resides in urban areas While 82% of Americans live in urban settings, only 30% of Indians do so This figure is predicted to climb as the percentage of Americans residing in rural areas falls by an average of 1.6% annually

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. I will not be able to ___________ my assignment in time
questions.explanation: C) ‘complete'का use होगा क्योंकि "complete" verb की base form है और future tense के वाक्य में 'will' के साथ हमेशा base form का ही use होता है। sentence में यह mention है कि वक्ता अपना असाइनमेंट समय पर पूरा नहीं कर पाएगा। जबकि 'completes' का use present tense में तीसरे व्यक्ति (he, she, it) के साथ होता है। 'completing' present participle है और इसका use continuous tense में होता है, जो यहाँ सही नहीं है। 'completed' past tense या past participle है, जो future tense में सही नहीं है। complete'will be used because it is the base form of the verb, and in future tense sentences, 'will' is followed by the base form. The sentence indicates that the speaker will not be able to finish their assignment on time. Whereas, 'completes' is used in present tense for the third person singular (he, she, it). 'Completing' is the present participle used in continuous tenses, which does not fit here. 'Completed' is the past tense or past participle and is not suitable for this future tense sentence.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined idiom/phrase in the given sentence. The salesman informed the customers that if they had any questions, they could drop a line.
questions.explanation: D)Drop a line(idiom) – To send a brief letter or note. एक छोटा सा पत्र या नोट भेजना।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. As 'brutal' is synonymous to 'harsh', 'naive' is synonymous to ________.
questions.explanation: A)'Simple'का use होगा क्योंकि 'naive' का अर्थ है भोला या सीधा-सादा, जो 'simple' का synonym है। जबकि 'worldly' का अर्थ है दुनियादार, 'wise' का अर्थ है बुद्धिमान, और 'hospitable' का अर्थ है आतिथ्यपूर्ण, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। 'Simple'will be used because 'naive' means innocent or straightforward, which is synonymous with 'simple'. The question discusses the synonym relationship between the given words. Whereas, 'worldly' means sophisticated or experienced, 'wise' means intelligent, and 'hospitable' means welcoming or generous, none of which fit in this context.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. My cousin thinks that / English is difficult / than other subjects
questions.explanation: C) ‘difficult' के बदले 'more difficult than other ' का use होगा क्योंकि Comparative Degree का use करते समय 'more' लगाना आवश्यक होता है जब adjective (जैसे: difficult) दो या अधिक syllables का हो। यहाँ 'difficult' three-syllable adjective है, इसलिए इसकी तुलना करते समय 'more difficult' का use किया जाएगा; जैसे— English is more difficult than Mathematics. ‘more difficult than other subjects’ will be used instead of ‘difficult than other subjects’ because when comparing using adjectives that have two or more syllables (like difficult), the comparative form is made by adding ‘more’ before the adjective. So, to make a grammatically correct comparative sentence, it should be: English is more difficult than other subjects; Like— English is more difficult than Mathematics.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The manager’s arbitrary decisions frustrated the employees.
questions.explanation: B) Arbitrary(adjective) – random, capricious, subjective, unjustified, despotic मनमाना Synonym: Random(adjective) – Arbitrary, chance, unplanned. (यादृच्छिक) Fair(adjective) – Just, impartial, unbiased. (निष्पक्ष) Justified(adjective) – Valid, reasonable, warranted. (उचित) Reasonable(adjective) – Logical, sensible, fair. (वाजिब)

questions.question: Select the correct spelling from the given options to fill in the blank. They may vary and seem very different, but they are not ________.
questions.explanation: C) “Irreconcilable" का अर्थ होता है "ऐसा जो समझौते या सामंजस्य के लिए संभव नहीं हो।" Sentence में यह बताया गया है कि वे भले ही अलग-अलग हों और भिन्न दिखें, परंतु वे irreconcilable नहीं हैं। इसका अर्थ है कि उनके बीच सामंजस्य या समाधान संभव है। इसलिए, यहाँ सही spelling "irreconcilable" होगी। "Irreconcilable" means "impossible to reconcile or bring into agreement." In the sentence, it is implied that while the subjects may differ or seem very different, they are not "irreconcilable," suggesting that reconciliation or harmony is possible. Thus, the correct spelling is"irreconcilable.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the highlighted group of words in the given sentence. RK Laxman was well-known for his drawings of people in such a highly exaggerated manner as to cause laughter.
questions.explanation: D)Caricatures(noun) – A picture, description, or imitation of a person in which certain striking characteristics are exaggerated to create a comic or grotesque effect. हास्यचित्र Sketches(noun) – A simple, quickly made drawing without much detail. रेखाचित्र Articles(noun) – A piece of writing included with others in a newspaper, magazine, or journal. लेख Portraits(noun) – A painting, drawing, or photograph of a person, typically showing only the face or head and shoulders. व्यक्ति का चित्र

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given underlined word. The resultant effect of the heavy rains was severe flooding in the city.
questions.explanation: B) Resultant(adjective) – consequent, ensuing, subsequent, following, outcome-based परिणामी Synonym: Ensuing(adjective) – Happening as a result (आगामी / परिणामस्वरूप) Initial(adjective) – Existing at the beginning (प्रारंभिक) Primary(adjective) – Most important (प्राथमिक) Original(adjective) – First of its kind (मूल)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To throw caution to the wind
options: To do something with care and attention
To give answer with utmost attention
To roam here and there aimlessly
To do something without worrying about risk
questions.explanation: D)To throw caution to the wind(idiom) – To do something without worrying about risk "जोखिम की परवाह किए बिना कुछ करना।"

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Whenever she is coming / here, she brings / many gifts for everyone.
questions.explanation: C) "Whenever she is coming" में error है। 'Whenever' का use आदतन या बार-बार होने वाली क्रिया के लिए किया जाता है। ऐसी स्थिति में Present Continuous Tense (is coming) की बजाय Simple Present Tense (comes) का use किया जाता है। अतः "she is coming" को "she comes" से बदलना होगा। सही वाक्य: "Whenever she comes here, she brings many gifts for everyone." The error lies in part C: "Whenever she is coming." The word "Whenever" indicates a habitual or repeated action. For such actions, the present continuous tense ("she is coming") is not appropriate; instead, the simple present tense ("she comes") should be used. Correct Sentence: "Whenever she comes here, she brings many gifts for everyone.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. The government introduced new policies to curb the rising pollution levels in the city.
questions.explanation: A) Curb(verb) – restrain, limit, control, restrict, suppress रोक लगाना Antonym: Encourage(verb) – To stimulate, support, or promote (प्रोत्साहित करना) Restrict(verb) – To limit or control (प्रतिबंधित करना) Suppress(verb) – To forcibly put an end to (दबाना) Control(verb) – To regulate or command (नियंत्रित करना)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Pull someone’s leg
options: To tease or play a joke on someone
To take a situation seriously or earnestly
To physically pull the legs of a person
questions.explanation: B)Pull someone’s leg (Idiom)– To tease or play a joke on someone किसी का मजाक बनाना या छेड़ना।

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. She often goes for a walk / with her dog / in the night.
options: She often goes for a walk
questions.explanation: A) 'in the night' में error है क्योंकि सही phrase 'at night' होगा। 'In the night' का USE तब किया जाता है जब किसी specific night का जिक्र हो, जबकि सामान्य तौर पर रात के समय (general nighttime) के लिए 'at night' का use होता है। The phrase 'in the night' is incorrect in this context because it is not the appropriate idiomatic expression for general nighttime activities. The correct phrase is 'at night'. The preposition 'in' is used for specific parts of the night (e.g., in the middle of the night) but not for general references.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The US may want to pass laws about (A) / Hong Kong’s special position, but with (B) / US and China now at logerheads, Hong Kong residents (C) / will have to decide where their ultimate loyalty lies. (D)
options: The US may want to pass laws about
Hong Kong’s special position, but with
US and China now at logerheads, Hong Kong residents
will have to decide where their ultimate loyalty lies.
questions.explanation: C) logerheads' के बदले 'loggerheads' का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि 'loggerheads' सही स्पेलिंग है जिसका अर्थ है 'गंभीर विवाद या संघर्ष'। 'loggerheads' will be used instead of 'logerheads' because 'loggerheads' is the correct spelling which means 'a state of open dispute or conflict'.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. Whether it is scorching summers or bone-chilling winters, Q. In India, tea transcends mere consumption; it embodies a lifestyle R. Whether in the bustling streets of Mumbai or the serene backwaters of Kerala, the clinking of tea cups resonates, marking the essence of every region in the country S. tea is a remedy that brings Indians together to start the day, refuel, or simply enjoy a moment of solace
questions.explanation: C) QRPS Q sentence paragraph की शुरुआत करता है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि “In India, tea transcends mere consumption; it embodies a lifestyle” यानी भारत में चाय सिर्फ एक पेय नहीं है, यह एक lifestyle का हिस्सा है। यह पूरे paragraph का main idea सेट करता है और चाय की cultural significance को introduce करता है। R logically Q के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह उदाहरण देता है कि भारत के अलग-अलग हिस्सों में, जैसे Mumbai की सड़कों से लेकर Kerala के backwaters तक, लोग चाय का आनंद कैसे लेते हैं। यह Q में बताई गई cultural significance को विस्तार देता है और specific regions के examples के ज़रिए इस विचार को support करता है। P R के बाद आता है क्योंकि यह बताता है कि चाय हर मौसम में एक remedy है, चाहे वह गर्मी हो या सर्दी। “Whether it is scorching summers or bone-chilling winters,” यह sentence tea की importance को seasons के साथ जोड़ता है, जो कि पहले regions की बात कर रहे थे, अब seasons की बात शुरू होती है। S sentence paragraph को logically conclude करता है क्योंकि यह summarize करता है कि चाय कैसे लोगों को जोड़ती है — “tea is a remedy that brings Indians together to start the day, refuel, or simply enjoy a moment of solace.” यह sentence पूरे paragraph की बातों को जोड़कर एक coherent conclusion देता है। Explanation in English: Q + R: R supports Q by providing regional examples of the cultural importance of tea mentioned in Q. R + P: P builds on R by talking about tea’s role in different weather conditions, extending the cultural discussion from regions to seasons. P + S: S concludes the paragraph by summarizing the unifying nature of tea, connecting back to the idea introduced in P about how tea is enjoyed regardless of the weather.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help ofthe alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. She was, I think, a good woman – and she had him sent (1) _______. When her husband died, she (2) _________ the truth. And so, to save the man she loved, she tried to destroy the (3) ________ against him. Later, I think, he (4) _________ her that her suspicions were (5) _________, and she consented to marry him Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1.
questions.explanation: B)Away'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि 'sent away' का अर्थ होता है किसी व्यक्ति को कहीं और भेजना। वाक्य में यह दर्शाया गया है कि महिला ने उसे कहीं और भेज दिया था, और 'away' इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त है। जबकि 'on' का अर्थ होता है किसी चीज़ पर, 'by' का अर्थ होता है किसी के द्वारा, और 'through' का अर्थ है किसी प्रक्रिया के माध्यम से, जो इस वाक्य के संदर्भ में सही नहीं हैं। Away'will be used because 'sent away' means to send someone to a different place. The sentence indicates that the woman had him sent somewhere else, making 'away' the correct choice. Whereas, 'on' means on something, 'by' refers to someone’s action, and 'through' implies a process, which do not fit this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help ofthe alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. She was, I think, a good woman – and she had him sent (1) _______. When her husband died, she (2) _________ the truth. And so, to save the man she loved, she tried to destroy the (3) ________ against him. Later, I think, he (4) _________ her that her suspicions were (5) _________, and she consented to marry him Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.
questions.explanation: B)Suspected'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि "suspected" का अर्थ होता है शक करना या संदेह करना। वाक्य में mention किया गया है कि उसके पति की मृत्यु के बाद, उसने सच्चाई का पता लगाया और संदिग्ध होने का संकेत दिया गया है। इसलिए 'suspected' यहाँ सही उत्तर है। जबकि 'Smuggled' का अर्थ है चोरी-छुपे कुछ स्थानांतरित करना, 'Debited' का अर्थ है किसी खाते से राशि काटना, और 'Filed' का अर्थ है किसी दस्तावेज को प्रस्तुत करना या दायर करना, जो इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं हैं Suspected'will be used because it means to doubt or have suspicion. The sentence mentions that after her husband died, she discovered the truth, indicating suspicion. Hence, 'suspected' is the most appropriate choice here. Whereas, 'Smuggled' means to move something secretly, 'Debited' means to deduct an amount from an account, and 'Filed' means to submit or present a document, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help ofthe alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. She was, I think, a good woman – and she had him sent (1) _______. When her husband died, she (2) _________ the truth. And so, to save the man she loved, she tried to destroy the (3) ________ against him. Later, I think, he (4) _________ her that her suspicions were (5) _________, and she consented to marry him Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.
questions.explanation: C)Evidence'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ होता है प्रमाण या गवाही। वाक्य यह दर्शाता है कि उसने उस व्यक्ति के खिलाफ प्रमाण को नष्ट करने की कोशिश की थी जिसे वह प्यार करती थी। Time' का अर्थ है समय, जो इस संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'Crime' का अर्थ है अपराध, लेकिन यहाँ संदर्भ में नष्ट करने की कोशिश की बात हो रही है, जो 'crime' के लिए प्रासंगिक नहीं है। 'Opportunity' का अर्थ है अवसर, जो यहाँ संदर्भित नहीं है। Evidence'will be used because it means proof or testimony. The sentence indicates that she tried to destroy the evidence against the man she loved. 'Time' means a period, which doesn't fit in this context. 'Crime' refers to an offense, but the context is about trying to destroy something, which is not relevant to 'crime'. 'Opportunity' means a chance, which is not indicated here.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help ofthe alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. She was, I think, a good woman – and she had him sent (1) _______. When her husband died, she (2) _________ the truth. And so, to save the man she loved, she tried to destroy the (3) ________ against him. Later, I think, he (4) _________ her that her suspicions were (5) _________, and she consented to marry him Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.
questions.explanation: B)'Persuaded'का use सही होगा क्योंकि 'persuaded' का अर्थ होता है किसी को कुछ करने के लिए मनाना या राजी करना। वाक्य describe करता है कि बाद में उसने उसे यह समझाया कि उसकी शंकाएँ गलत थीं, और उसने उससे शादी करने के लिए सहमति व्यक्त की। 'Guessed' का अर्थ केवल अनुमान लगाना होता है, जो इस संदर्भ में फिट नहीं बैठता। 'Killed' का अर्थ है हत्या करना, जो यहाँ बिल्कुल भी प्रासंगिक नहीं है। 'Pondered' का अर्थ है विचार करना, जो इस संदर्भ में सही नहीं बैठता। 'Persuaded'will be the correct choice because it means to convince or induce someone to do something. The sentence implies that later he convinced her that her suspicions were unfounded, and she agreed to marry him. 'Guessed' merely means to estimate, which doesn’t fit here. 'Killed' means to murder, which is irrelevant in this context. 'Pondered' means to think deeply, which doesn’t align with the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help ofthe alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each blank. She was, I think, a good woman – and she had him sent (1) _______. When her husband died, she (2) _________ the truth. And so, to save the man she loved, she tried to destroy the (3) ________ against him. Later, I think, he (4) _________ her that her suspicions were (5) _________, and she consented to marry him Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
questions.explanation: D)Unfounded'का use किया जाएगा क्योंकि इसका अर्थ है "बिना किसी आधार के" या "ग़लत सिद्ध होना।" sentence में यह बताया गया है कि उस व्यक्ति ने महिला को यह समझाया कि उसके संदेह ग़लत थे, इसलिए 'unfounded' यहाँ सही विकल्प है। True' का अर्थ है सत्य, लेकिन यहाँ संदर्भ में यह फिट नहीं होता। 'Facts' का अर्थ है तथ्य, लेकिन संदेह तथ्य नहीं थे, इसलिए यह विकल्प सही नहीं है। 'Concrete' का अर्थ है ठोस या निश्चित, जो यहाँ संदर्भ में उपयुक्त नहीं है 'Unfounded' will be used because it means "without basis" or "proven false." The sentence indicates that the man convinced her that her suspicions were incorrect, making 'unfounded' the most appropriate option. True' means "accurate or correct," but it does not fit the context of disproving suspicions. 'Facts' refers to "verifiable information," but the suspicions were not facts, so this option is incorrect. 'Concrete' means "definite or solid," which does not align with the idea of disproved suspicions.
